what_it_does: A self-contained design-system preview card rendering the product sidebar navigation in its three row states (default/hover/active), with section glyphs drawn from the CV icon library and a side legend explaining each state.
observations: Carries the @dsCard metadata comment (group Brand, name 'Sidebar nav states', viewport 700x320).
Loads ../assets/icons/cv-icons.js and builds nav rows in script from a JS ITEMS array.
Each item declares label, icon name, a hardcoded section hue, and state (default/hover/active).
State styles: default transparent; hover white --bg-surface + --shadow-xs; active white fill + 1.5px dashed --border-dashed (the signature selection treatment).
Icons rendered as inline SVG (viewBox 0 0 24 24, stroke-width 1.7) with body pulled from window.CV_ICONS.data[name]; stroke colour set per-item.
Section hues hardcoded inline: teal #2BA39A, charcoal #3A3026, blue #3B7DD8, gold #C8AB0E, pink #D8568A.
Legend describes Icons/Default/Hover/Active in prose; comment notes hues were sampled from the product dashboard reference.
standouts: Dashed-gold active outline is named 'the brand's signature selection treatment'.
Per-section icon tinting (each glyph carries its own hue) is an explicit design rule, not decoration.
conventions: Follows token references for surface/shadow/border/radius and the shared CV icon library.
Breaks token discipline with the five hardcoded section hue hexes inline in the script.
Reuses the dashed-gold active treatment consistent with the buttons/cards dashed language.
concerns: Section colour hues are hardcoded hex literals in JS rather than token references.
notes: Sidebar nav preview showing default/hover/active row states with per-section tinted CV-library icons; the active state is the signature white-fill + dashed-gold outline.
questions: Should the per-section hues (teal/charcoal/blue/gold/pink) be promoted to named tokens rather than inline hexes?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — declares sidebar nav states preview and renders the three states with coloured CV icons and a legend.
intention_signals: To codify how sidebar rows look across interaction states and to assert the CV icon library with section-specific hues as the canonical nav iconography.
novelty: The combination of per-section hue tinting on shared line icons plus the dashed-gold active outline as the selection signature.
contribution: Contributes the sidebar nav state vocabulary (default/hover/active row styling) and the per-section tinted-icon convention drawn from the CV icon library.
summary_for_embedding: A token-driven HTML preview card rendering sidebar nav rows in default/hover/active states with per-section tinted CV-library line icons and a state legend.
intention_for_embedding: To present the design system's sidebar navigation states and per-section icon-tinting convention for visual review in the gallery.
